title: Incident Record
description: Single record from an incident JSONL file (one file per incident under framework-memory/incidents/<YYYY-MM>/INC-<ulid>.jsonl). Phase 4 contract for the records that hooks._incident.write_incident() and hooks._incident.append_to_incident() produce. The first line of each file is an `initial record` (with `category`); subsequent lines are `follow-up records` (without `category` — the category was set when the incident was opened). Phase 10's closed-loop-incident-retrospective-analyst is the canonical consumer.

ulid.description: 26-character Crockford-base32 ULID. Stable across all records in the same incident file. Crockford excludes I, L, O, U for OCR-safety.

timestamp.description: Per-record ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at second precision. The first record's timestamp also encodes the file's creation moment; follow-up records carry their own timestamps so the file reads top-to-bottom as a chronological narrative.

category.description: Short kebab-case identifier set when the incident was opened (`stop-failure`, `permission-denied`, etc.). Present only on the initial record; follow-up records omit it because the category is fixed for the lifetime of the incident.

severity.description: Per-record severity. The initial record defaults to `error`; follow-ups default to `info`. Callers may override either.

detail.description: Human-readable summary or follow-up note. Empty strings are rejected at write time.

extra.description: Per-record event-specific fields. Contents are open-vocabulary — different categories carry different fields (tool_name, file_path, command, etc.). Always present, even when empty.
